perturbation.original_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon MSC with respect to the Information which (a) was in MSC's possession before receipt from the Company; (b) is or becomes a matter of public knowledge through no fault of MSC; (c) is rightfully received by MSC from a third party without a duty of confidentiality; (d) is disclosed by the Company to a third party without a duty of confidentiality on the third party; (e) is independently developed by MSC; (f) is disclosed under operation of law; or (g) is disclosed by MSC with the Company's prior approval.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon MSC with respect to the Information which (a) was in MSC's possession before receipt from the Company; (b) is or becomes a matter of public knowledge through no fault of MSC; (c) is rightfully received by MSC from a third party without a duty of confidentiality; (d) is disclosed by the Company to a third party without a duty of confidentiality on the third party; (e) is independently developed by MSC; (f) is disclosed under operation of law.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause (g) specifies that the agreement imposes no obligation upon MSC with respect to Information which is disclosed by MSC with the Company's prior approval. By removing this clause, the agreement now implies that even if MSC discloses the information with the company's prior approval, they still might be in violation of the confidentiality agreement. This introduces uncertainty and contradiction with the overall purpose of seeking prior approval for disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MSC recognizes that the Information constitutes a valuable property of the Company. MSC agrees that it will not permit any person, firm or corporation to use, copy or reproduce in any manner whatsoever any part of the Information, except to the extent that the Information must be reproduced by MSC in performing its data processing services.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes clause (g) from the original text, which stated that the agreement imposes no obligation on MSC if information is disclosed with the Company's prior approval. The contradicted text states that MSC agrees not to permit any person to use the information, except when reproduced for data processing services. By removing clause (g), the changed text creates a scenario where even with the company's prior approval, MSC might still be in violation of the confidentiality agreement described in the contradicted text, thus creating a direct contradiction and uncertainty.
